subject: English
chapter: Poems – How to Tell Wild Animals, The Ball Poem

activities_projects.title: In-depth Animal Behavior Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the poet’s detailed observations and literary depiction of animals.
steps: List all animals mentioned in 'How to Tell Wild Animals'
Analyze behavior, features, and implied symbolism for each animal
Write a 1–2 page report connecting animal traits to the poet’s intent and humor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking and interpretive skills for figurative language and satire.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, textual support, and insightfulness

activities_projects.title: Humor and Literary Devices Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine complex use of humor, irony, and satire in poetry.
steps: Identify instances of irony, exaggeration, and wordplay
Explain how these devices enhance humor and meaning
Discuss how tone changes between descriptive and humorous lines
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand advanced literary techniques and their effects on tone and theme.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of device identification and analytical depth

activities_projects.title: Theme Exploration Essay
activities_projects.objective: Analyze complex themes of loss, resilience, and maturity in 'The Ball Poem'.
steps: Identify multiple themes in the poem
Find lines supporting each theme and explain the poet’s technique
Write a structured essay analyzing the themes and their universal relevance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen essay writing, thematic interpretation, and critical analysis skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Coherence, depth, and use of textual evidence

activities_projects.title: Poetic Device Deep Dive
activities_projects.objective: Compare literary devices across both poems.
steps: List devices such as metaphor, imagery, personification, simile, alliteration
Explain how each device enhances meaning or tone
Compare their use between humorous and serious poems
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand nuanced poetic techniques and compare stylistic approaches.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, depth of explanation, and comparative insight

activities_projects.title: Critical Interpretation of Emotions
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the emotional depth and psychological insight in both poems.
steps: Track emotional progression in the boy from 'The Ball Poem'
Examine implied emotions in animal descriptions
Write an analytical paragraph on how emotions convey the underlying message
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop advanced comprehension and emotional-literary analysis skills.

activities_projects.title: Comparative Tone and Style Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand how tone and style differ between humorous and serious poems.
steps: Identify tone shifts in both poems
Analyze how word choice, sentence length, and imagery affect tone
Write a comparative note discussing effect on reader perception
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand literary tone, style, and its impact on interpretation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, textual references, and analytical depth

activities_projects.title: Advanced Imagery Illustration
steps: Select one stanza from each poem with rich imagery
Draw a detailed illustration representing literal and figurative meaning
Write a 3–4 sentence explanation of your visualization
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance creative interpretation of figurative language and imagery.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, depth of symbolism, and clarity of explanation

activities_projects.title: Extended Role-Play and Debate
steps: Act out the boy in 'The Ball Poem' losing the ball and explain thought process
Act out a humorous animal scenario
Debate in groups: How humor vs seriousness affects reader understanding
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance critical thinking, empathy, and oral communication skills.

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary in Context Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Apply advanced vocabulary and literary terms.
steps: Identify challenging words and poetic terms (personification, irony, satire)
Write meanings and use them in analytical sentences about the poems
Discuss how word choice contributes to tone and theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students expand vocabulary and connect language to literary analysis.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, contextual usage, and relevance to analysis

activities_projects.title: Comparative Literary Critique
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize understanding of style, theme, and literary techniques.
steps: Compare and contrast both poems in terms of theme, tone, and literary devices
Write a critical paragraph discussing effectiveness of the poet’s choices
Support arguments with textual evidence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop advanced analytical and evaluative skills in literature.
activities_projects.evaluation: Insightfulness, clarity, and strength of textual justification
